title: Course spine
description: Canonical structure for course/spine.yaml (or spine.json). Mirrors project-root CONTENTS.md: chapter headings and per-chapter lesson titles must match exactly.

course_id.description: Stable slug for the course (folder names, tooling).

audience_level.description: Target level, e.g. beginner, intermediate, advanced.

chapters.description: Linear chapter list; order is global reading/build order.

dir.description: Filesystem folder name under project root, e.g. Ch1, Ch2.

title.description: Chapter title; must match CONTENTS.md heading after '### {dir}: '.

lessons.description: Lessons in this chapter; ids are stems for Ch#/L# files.

lessonRef.description: Cross-chapter dependency: chapter dir + '/' + lesson id.

id.description: Lesson file stem inside chapter dir: L1, L2, …

title.description: Lesson title; must match numbered line in CONTENTS.md for this chapter.

concept.description: One concise concept taught this lesson.

project_step.description: One real project advance tied to this lesson.

reads.description: Optional paths or URLs for assigned reading.

test_glob.description: Glob or single path resolving to this lesson’s tests only. Canonical Python flow uses append-only `main_test.py`; legacy per-lesson files like Ch1/L1.py are migration-compatible.

lesson_selector.description: Pytest marker selector for this lesson when `test_glob` is `main_test.py`. Use `lesson_ch<chapter>_l<lesson>` (e.g. lesson_ch1_l2) and run with `uv run pytest main_test.py -m <lesson_selector>`.

depends_on.description: Prior lessons (Ch#/L# refs). Empty for the course’s first lesson(s).

hints.description: If true, Ch#/L#-hint.md must exist beside Ch#/L#.md.
